SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.automation

Class SensitivityAnalyzer

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.automation.SensitivityAnalyzer
public class SensitivityAnalyzer extends Object
Gradient and Jacobian computation over the agent-facing ProcessAutomation API.

Many downstream agentic workflows (sensitivity studies, gradient-based optimization, what-if scoping, KPI sensitivity dashboards) need partial derivatives of simulation outputs with respect to inputs without having to instrument every equipment class. This analyzer provides those derivatives by finite differences driven entirely through the existing string-addressable facade.

Two perturbation modes are supported:

  • Central difference (default): dy/dx ≈ (y(x+h) - y(x-h)) / (2 h) — O(h²) accurate, requires two re-runs per input.
  • Forward difference: dy/dx ≈ (y(x+h) - y(x)) / h — O(h) accurate, requires one re-run per input (cheaper for large Jacobians).

The step size for each input defaults to max(absStep, relStep · |x|) so it scales gracefully across pressures (bara) and temperatures (K). The original input value is restored after every probe so the underlying ProcessAutomation is left in the state it started in.

Example: 

 ProcessAutomation auto = process.getAutomation();
 SensitivityAnalyzer sens = new SensitivityAnalyzer(auto);
 double dPowerDPin = sens.partial("Compressor.inletStream.pressure", "bara", "Compressor.power", "kW");
 Map<String, Double> grad = sens.gradient("Compressor.power", "kW",
     Arrays.asList("Compressor.inletStream.pressure", "Compressor.outletPressure"), "bara");
Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

  • Constructor Details

    • SensitivityAnalyzer

      public SensitivityAnalyzer(ProcessAutomation automation)
      Creates an analyzer bound to a ProcessAutomation facade.
      Parameters:
      automation - non-null automation facade; must already be wired to a runnable process

  • Method Details

    • setRelativeStep

      public SensitivityAnalyzer setRelativeStep(double relativeStep)
      Sets the relative perturbation step. The actual step used per input is max(absoluteStep, relativeStep · |x|).
      Parameters:
      relativeStep - relative step (must be > 0); typical value 1e-4
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining

    • setAbsoluteStep

      public SensitivityAnalyzer setAbsoluteStep(double absoluteStep)
      Sets the absolute floor on perturbation step.
      Parameters:
      absoluteStep - absolute step (must be > 0); typical value 1e-6
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining

    • setMode

      public SensitivityAnalyzer setMode(SensitivityAnalyzer.Mode mode)
      Sets the finite-difference mode.
      Parameters:
      mode - non-null mode
      Returns:
      this analyzer for chaining

    • getRelativeStep

      public double getRelativeStep()
      Returns the configured relative step.
      Returns:
      relative step

    • getAbsoluteStep

      public double getAbsoluteStep()
      Returns the configured absolute step.
      Returns:
      absolute step

    • getMode

      public SensitivityAnalyzer.Mode getMode()
      Returns the configured finite-difference mode.
      Returns:
      mode

    • partial

      public double partial(String inputAddress, String inputUnit, String outputAddress, String outputUnit)
      Computes the partial derivative of a single output with respect to a single input by finite differences. The underlying process is re-run via ProcessAutomation.run() after each perturbation and is restored to its original value afterwards.
      Parameters:
      inputAddress - dot-notation address of the input variable (must be an INPUT-type variable)
      inputUnit - unit string for the input value, e.g. "bara"; may be null or empty for default
      outputAddress - dot-notation address of the output variable
      outputUnit - unit string for the output value, e.g. "kW"; may be null or empty for default
      Returns:
      ∂(output)/∂(input) in outputUnit / inputUnit; returns Double.NaN when either probe failed

    • gradient

      public Map<String,Double> gradient(String outputAddress, String outputUnit, List<String> inputAddresses, String inputUnit)
      Computes the gradient ∇y of a single output with respect to a list of inputs. Each input is perturbed independently with the original values restored between probes.
      Parameters:
      outputAddress - dot-notation address of the output variable
      outputUnit - unit string for the output value; may be null or empty for default
      inputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of input addresses
      inputUnit - unit string applied to all input values; may be null or empty for default
      Returns:
      ordered map keyed by input address with ∂y/∂x in each entry

    • jacobian

      public double[][] jacobian(List<String> outputAddresses, String outputUnit, List<String> inputAddresses, String inputUnit)
      Computes the Jacobian matrix ∂y_i/∂x_j over a list of outputs and inputs. Rows correspond to outputs and columns to inputs.
      Parameters:
      outputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of output addresses
      outputUnit - unit string applied to all output values; may be null or empty for default
      inputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of input addresses
      inputUnit - unit string applied to all input values; may be null or empty for default
      Returns:
      Jacobian matrix of shape [outputs.size()][inputs.size()]

    • gradientAsJson

      public String gradientAsJson(String outputAddress, String outputUnit, List<String> inputAddresses, String inputUnit)
      Returns a JSON representation of a gradient including the schema version, output address, input addresses and computed derivatives. Useful as a stable agent handoff format.
      Parameters:
      outputAddress - dot-notation address of the output variable
      outputUnit - unit string for the output value; may be null or empty for default
      inputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of input addresses
      inputUnit - unit string applied to all input values; may be null or empty for default
      Returns:
      JSON string with keys schemaVersion, mode, output, outputUnit, inputUnit, and gradient

    • jacobianAsJson

      public String jacobianAsJson(List<String> outputAddresses, String outputUnit, List<String> inputAddresses, String inputUnit)
      Returns a JSON representation of a Jacobian.
      Parameters:
      outputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of output addresses
      outputUnit - unit string applied to all output values; may be null or empty for default
      inputAddresses - non-null, non-empty list of input addresses
      inputUnit - unit string applied to all input values; may be null or empty for default
      Returns:
      JSON string with keys schemaVersion, mode, outputs, inputs, outputUnit, inputUnit, and jacobian (a 2D array indexed as [outputIndex][inputIndex])

    • readAll

      private double[] readAll(List<String> addresses, String unit)

    • getInputAddresses

      public List<String> getInputAddresses()
      Convenience: returns the input addresses currently held (none — kept for API symmetry).
      Returns:
      empty list (this analyzer is stateless w.r.t. addresses)
